title: India’s $2 Billion State Stake Sales Buck Equity Market Slowdown
summary: India’s federal government raised $2 billion through stake sales in listed state-run companies last quarter, providing a rare bright spot for the country’s subdued capital market after the conflict in the Middle East slowed broader equity issuance.

title: Thailand Gets Serious About Chasing High-Spending Tourists
summary: Thailand is done chasing tourist records. After decades of measuring success by ever-rising arrival numbers, the government is making its clearest push yet to prioritize how much visitors spend versus how many show up.

title: Singapore Home Prices Rise for Seventh Quarter; Pace Slows
summary: Singapore private home prices rose for a seventh straight quarter as strong demand for new units cushioned the market from the effects of the Iran war.

title: Global M&A Tops $2.5 Trillion After First-Half Deals Surge
summary: The year was tipped to be a potential blockbuster in deals and the first half delivered, setting a pace that is likely to continue in the closing months of 2026.

title: Trump May Ask Supreme Court to Reconsider Carroll Appeal Denial
summary: President Donald Trump may ask the US Supreme Court to reconsider its decision to turn away his appeal of a $5 million verdict against him in E. Jean Carroll’s sexual-abuse lawsuit, her lawyer said in a court filing asking for an immediate payout of the damages award.

title: India Prop Traders Brace for RBI Funding Squeeze
summary: Traders expect reduced returns from cash-futures arbitrage, options market making and index arbitrage.

title: Panasonic Targets Further AI Growth to Build on Record Valuation
summary: Panasonic Holdings Corp., the Japanese electronics conglomerate, is attracting renewed investor attention as a beneficiary of the artificial-intelligence boom.

title: Coles in Talks to Buy Pet Shop and Vet Business Greencross
summary: Coles Group Ltd. said it’s in talks to buy pet store and veterinary business Greencross Pet Wellness Co., a deal that reportedly could be valued at as much as A$4 billion ($2.8 billion).

title: Australia Proposes Consulting Sector Overhaul After Scandals
summary: Australia proposed sweeping changes to the consulting sector including new powers to police the industry and impose larger fines for poor behavior, following a string of scandals in recent years.

title: Uber-Backed Bike Firm Lime, Investors Raise $174 Million in IPO
summary: Neutron Holdings Inc., better known as Lime, and some of its shareholders raised $174 million in a US initial public offering that priced at the midpoint of the marketed range.

summary: Bending Spoons SpA, which acquires struggling software businesses, and some of its backers raised $1.68 billion in a US initial public offering priced above a marketed range.

title: Goldman Flags Up Oil Surplus Even as Nations Rebuild Stockpiles
summary: The global oil market is set to swing back into oversupply as the impact of the Iran war fades and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz recovers, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

title: Primark’s Sales Fall Before Fashion Chain’s Split from AB Foods
summary: Associated British Foods Plc reported falling sales at its Primark budget fashion chain, as the conglomerate prepares to separate its clothing and food divisions.

title: Jardine Matheson Taps Ex-Indonesian Fund CIO as Country Head
summary: Jardine Matheson Holdings has hired Christopher Ganis, formerly chief investment officer at sovereign wealth fund Indonesia Investment Authority, as Indonesia country head, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Gold Drops for Third Day as Jitters Over US Rate Outlook Worsen
summary: Gold fell for a third day as signals the Federal Reserve may move to tighten policy aided the dollar and traders tracked US-Iran talks.

title: Europe’s Bank Bulls See More Upside After 21% Quarterly Gain
summary: Following a stellar second-quarter rally for European banks, investors expect one of the region’s hottest equity trades to keep delivering.

title: Abu Dhabi’s MGX Raises $49 Billion for One of the Biggest Ever AI Funds
summary: MGX has raised $49 billion for one of the biggest ever funds dedicated to artificial intelligence deals, propelling the two-year-old Abu Dhabi firm into the ranks of the most consequential investors in the sector globally.

title: Dollar Gains Ahead of Warsh Speech, Gold Declines: Markets Wrap
summary: The dollar strengthened against all its Group-of-10 peers as traders awaited key speeches from a swath of central bankers, including Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, just as bets increased for an interest-rate hike.

title: Japan’s FX Chief Says Past Yen Intervention Has Shown Impact
summary: Japan’s currency chief said intervention to prop up the yen two months ago was successful and some US authorities voiced support, suggesting he views the move as an effective strategy as the yen hovers at a four-decade low.

title: FTSE 100 Set to Fall to Kick Off Second Half
Abu Dhabi Proposes New Offshore-Oil Pricing
summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co., the UAE’s biggest oil company, is proposing a new pricing methodology for the sale of crude supplies to term customers, opting for an approach that’s more aligned with wider regional trading norms, according to people with direct knowledge of the matter. Bloomberg's Anthony di Paola reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Homegrown AlphaGrep Takes on Jio BlackRock With India Algo Funds
summary: High-speed trader AlphaGrep Securities Pvt.’s asset management unit will offer algorithmic-driven trading strategies to India’s retail investors, according to a top executive, pitting it against the likes of Jio BlackRock Asset Management Pvt. for a slice of the nearly $900 billion mutual fund industry.

title: Cheveley: Gold to Recover Over the Next Few Months
summary: The price of gold slipped for a third day as traders tracked US-Iran peace talks and weighed signals the Federal Reserve may move to tighten policy. But George Cheveley, Natural Resources Portfolio Manager at Ninety One told Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East & Africa the yellow metal will bounce back soon. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: South Africa Plans to Tap Existing Rand Sukuk Bonds This Year
summary: South Africa will tap existing rand-denominated sukuk bonds as part of its funding plans for the current fiscal year.

title: Traders on Lookout for Next Yen 'Red Line' | The Asia Trade 7/1/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Steadies as Peace Talks Continue, Hormuz Traffic Recovers
summary: Oil steadied after the biggest quarterly drop since the pandemic, as traders monitored peace talks between the US and Iran and the return of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: India’s Below-Normal July Rain Forecast Risks Crop Outlook
summary: India is likely to receive below-normal rainfall in July, the monsoon’s wettest month, after its driest June in 12 years, threatening to further delay crop planting across large parts of the country.

title: RBI’s Short Dollar Book Hit Record in May Before Policy Measures
summary: The Reserve Bank of India’s short dollar book rose to a record $106.7 billion in May as the central bank used derivatives to defend the currency.

title: In Hindsight, Will a Coming Crash Seem Obvious?
summary: The template for 2008 holds up after a midyear check on our imaginary hedge fund.

title: Perpetual Shares Surge as Company Reveals Potential Takeover Bid
summary: Perpetual Ltd shares jumped before the company requested a pause in trading citing an approach over a potential takeover deal.

title: Aluminum Falls to Lowest Since February as Dollar Strengthens
summary: Aluminum fell to the lowest since mid-February as a strengthening US dollar continued to pressure commodities.

title: Chinese Inverter Maker Sungrow Plunges 20% on Potential US Ban
summary: Chinese inverter manufacturer Sungrow Power Supply Co. tumbled on Wednesday following a report that the US regulator is drafting a ban on imports due to national security concerns.

title: Foreign Buying of Indian Bonds Hits Record After Tax Break
summary: India’s bonds open to unrestricted global investment attracted a record monthly inflow after the government scrapped taxes on foreign investors.

title: Won Slides Toward Weakest Since 2009 as Global Funds Sell Stocks
summary: South Korea’s won slid toward its weakest level since the global financial crisis, leading a retreat in Asian currencies, as the dollar strengthened and overseas investors sold local equities.

title: US Lifts Export Restrictions on Anthropic’s Fable 5
summary: The US government removed foreign access restrictions on Anthropic PBC’s Fable 5 artificial intelligence model, clearing it for wider distribution after the startup resolved the Trump administration’s safety concerns. Bloomberg's Minmin Low breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Reports $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings
summary: President Donald Trump reported earning at least $1.4 billion in 2025 from crypto and memecoin-related businesses, according to his latest annual financial disclosure. Bloomberg's Kailey Leinz and Romaine Bostick break it down. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Alcoa Bets on Aluminum Boom With $5.6 Billion South32 Deal
summary: Alcoa Corp. has agreed to buy South32 Ltd.’s bauxite, alumina and aluminum assets in a deal worth as much as $5.6 billion, cementing its position as a top producer as long-term demand strengthens and the Iran war exposes supply concerns.

title: Mizuho Says Historic Slump in Yen Is Defying the Rates Rulebook
summary: The slump in the yen to historic lows is forcing investors to rethink one of their most widely used strategies to gauge the currency’s moves, according to Mizuho Bank Ltd.
